subject: Statistics
title: The M Competitions
description: Learn The M Competitions in one minute.

hook.text: Since 1982, the M Competitions have tested forecasting methods head-to-head on thousands of real time series.

buildup.text: Spyros Makridakis organized the competitions to settle debates about which methods actually work best.

discovery.text: Simple methods often match or beat complex ones. Combining forecasts from multiple methods usually wins.

twist.text: M4 in 2018 showed hybrid methods combining statistical and ML approaches outperformed either alone.

climax.text: M5 in 2020 used Walmart sales data. The winners combined gradient boosting with traditional time series models.

punchline.text: Competition settles arguments. Ensembles win and simplicity holds its own.

quiz.question: What has the M Competitions consistently shown about forecasting methods?
options: Combining forecasts from multiple methods typically outperforms any single method
Complex methods always beat simple ones
Only neural networks produce accurate forecasts
